mom.focus: Uterine recovery, colostrum support, iron replenishment. Keep everything warm and easy to digest.

breakfast.name: Methi kheer (fenugreek milk pudding)
breakfast.why: Fenugreek triggers prolactin — your milk-making hormone. Warm milk soothes the uterus.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Dry-roast 1 tsp methi seeds, grind to powder. Boil with 1 cup full-fat milk, 1 tsp ghee, jaggery to taste.

lunch.name: Moong dal khichdi with a big spoon of ghee
lunch.why: Easiest protein to digest right after birth. Ghee lubricates the gut, reduces constipation risk.
lunch.recipe_tip: Use yellow moong dal (skinless). Add cumin, turmeric, a pinch of hing. Top with warm ghee at the table.

dinner.name: Drumstick leaf soup + soft rice
dinner.why: Drumstick leaves are the richest plant source of calcium and iron — critical for post-birth recovery.
dinner.recipe_tip: Sauté drumstick leaves with garlic in ghee. Add water, boil 10 mins, strain or serve whole.

snacks: Warm ajwain (carom seed) water — reduces gas and bloating

foods_to_avoid: Cold water or cold drinks
Raw salads and raw vegetables
Cabbage, cauliflower, broccoli — can cause gas passed to baby via milk
Spicy curries — wait until day 7+
mom.hydration_tip: 6–8 glasses of warm water daily. Try jeera (cumin) water or ajwain water — both reduce postpartum bloating.
mom.traditional_note: South India: Pongal with ghee + drumstick sambar is the classic Day 1 meal. North India: Panjeeri (whole wheat + ghee + dry fruits) given from day 1. East India: Khichuri (rice + lentils) with posto (poppy seeds) for pain relief.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is getting colostrum — thick, golden-yellow, small in quantity. This is perfect. Colostrum is so nutrient-dense that even 1–2 teaspoons per feed is enough. Baby's stomach is the size of a marble today.
signs_feeding_well: Baby swallows after every few sucks — you can hear a soft 'kuh' sound
At least 1–2 wet diapers in first 24 hours
Baby seems calm and relaxed after feeding
signs_to_watch: Fewer than 1 wet diaper in 24 hours
Baby seems very lethargic and won't wake to feed
Baby is losing weight beyond 7% of birth weight
baby.latch_tip: Baby's mouth should cover most of the areola (dark area), not just the nipple. A wide, deep latch prevents sore nipples. If it hurts after the first 30 seconds, gently break the latch with your finger and try again.

mom.focus: Continue warm, soft foods. Iron-rich ingredients are priority. Avoid anything that causes gas.

breakfast.name: Ragi porridge with jaggery and ghee
breakfast.why: Ragi (finger millet) is the highest calcium grain — critical when you're breastfeeding. Jaggery replenishes iron lost during birth.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Mix 2 tbsp ragi flour in cold water, then stir into boiling water. Cook 5 min, add ghee + jaggery.

lunch.name: Masoor dal (red lentil) soup + soft chapati with ghee
lunch.why: Masoor dal is the fastest-cooking, easiest-to-digest lentil. High in folate and iron.
lunch.recipe_tip: Pressure cook masoor dal until very soft. Temper with ghee, cumin, garlic. Skip chilli today.

dinner.name: Bottle gourd (lauki) sabzi + rice
dinner.why: Lauki is highly hydrating, cooling (balances post-birth heat), and easy on the gut.
dinner.recipe_tip: Cook lauki soft with minimal spices — just cumin, turmeric, salt in ghee.

snacks: Halim (garden cress) seeds in warm milk — iron booster
Dry fruit ladoo with gondh (edible gum) for back support

foods_to_avoid: Maida (refined flour) products
Cold or refrigerated foods
Tea and coffee — both reduce iron absorption
mom.hydration_tip: Try saunf (fennel seed) water — boil 1 tsp fennel in 2 cups water, cool to warm, sip through the day. Helps milk let-down and reduces infant gas.
mom.traditional_note: Gondh ke ladoo (edible gum + whole wheat + ghee) are a North Indian staple from day 2 onwards — excellent for back pain and lactation. Ask your family to prepare a batch.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is losing some birth weight today — this is completely normal and expected. Up to 7% weight loss is standard. Baby's stomach has grown to the size of a walnut.
signs_feeding_well: Dark meconium (tar-like black stools) starting to lighten to green
signs_to_watch: Still no wet diapers
Baby seems too sleepy to feed — set an alarm to wake baby every 2 hours
baby.latch_tip: Try the laid-back feeding position if you're sore — lie back at 45°, let baby lie on your chest belly-down. Gravity helps them latch deeply.

mom.focus: Day 3 milk surge — your breasts may feel engorged and hard. This is normal. Feed frequently. Stay hydrated.

breakfast.name: Sabudana (sago) khichdi with peanuts and ghee
breakfast.why: Quick energy, easy digestion. Sago is soothing on an engorged day when stress is high.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Soak sabudana overnight. Cook with roasted peanuts, cumin, green chilli optional, ghee. Lemon juice at the end.

lunch.name: Palak (spinach) dal + rice + papad
lunch.why: Spinach is your highest plant-based iron source. Pair with vitamin C foods to boost absorption.
lunch.recipe_tip: Blend cooked spinach into toor dal. Add tomato for vitamin C. Avoid raw spinach — cook it fully.

dinner.name: Chicken or mutton bone broth + soft rice (non-veg) / Paneer bhurji + roti (veg)
dinner.why: Bone broth is collagen-rich — supports perineal and abdominal tissue healing. Paneer gives protein + calcium for veg moms.
dinner.recipe_tip: For broth: slow-cook bones 4–6 hours with ginger, garlic, turmeric. Strain and sip warm.

foods_to_avoid: Skip citrus fruits today if baby seems gassy
Avoid too much fiber — focus on gentle, cooked foods
mom.hydration_tip: On engorgement day, drink MORE water — 8–10 glasses. Dehydration worsens engorgement and reduces milk supply.
mom.traditional_note: Day 3 engorgement is the #1 reason mothers think their milk is 'too much' or 'too little' — it's neither. It's your body calibrating. This passes by day 5–7.

baby.feeds_per_day: 8–12 feeds — may feel like constant feeding today
baby.what_to_expect: Transitional milk is coming in — yellow-white, more fluid than colostrum. Baby may be fussier and feed more frequently. This is called cluster feeding and it's completely normal — baby is stimulating your supply.
signs_feeding_well: Stools changing from black/green to yellow-mustard
Baby feeds, seems satisfied, sleeps, wakes to feed again
signs_to_watch: Fewer than 4 wet diapers
Baby crying inconsolably between feeds even after feeding
baby.latch_tip: On engorgement days, soften the areola before feeding — hand-express a little milk, or use a warm cloth for 2 minutes. A hard, swollen breast makes latching harder for baby.

mom.focus: Keep warm. Rest between feeds. Let family do everything else.

breakfast.name: Bajra (pearl millet) khichdi with ghee
breakfast.why: Bajra is warming, iron-rich, and builds stamina for sleep-deprived nights.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Pressure cook bajra with moong dal, salt, turmeric. Top generously with ghee.

lunch.name: Arhar (toor) dal + rice + any soft vegetable
lunch.why: Toor dal is your everyday protein base. Rotate vegetables to prevent boredom.
lunch.recipe_tip: Keep cooking simple — pressure cook dal, add ghee tadka with cumin, garlic.

dinner.name: Suji (semolina) upma with vegetables
dinner.why: Easy digestion, comforting, filling without being heavy.
dinner.recipe_tip: Use fine suji. Add carrots, peas, cashews. Light ghee tadka with mustard seeds.

snacks: Almond and walnut mix (5 almonds + 3 walnuts) — brain-supportive omega-3
Warm milk with haldi and a pinch of black pepper

foods_to_avoid: Street food or outside food — still too soon
Cold drinks and ice cream
mom.hydration_tip: Keep a water bottle with you during every feed. You may feel suddenly very thirsty while breastfeeding — this is normal. Your body triggers thirst during milk let-down.
mom.traditional_note: The 'no cold food' rule in most Indian traditions exists because cold constricts blood vessels — which can slow uterine recovery and milk let-down. It's science wrapped in tradition.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby's stools should be transitioning to yellow-mustard by today. Weight loss peaks around day 3–4 and starts recovering from day 5.
signs_feeding_well: Baby's skin colour looks healthy, not overly yellow
signs_to_watch: Deep yellow/orange skin (jaundice) — common, usually harmless, but check with doctor if spreading below chest
Baby refusing both breasts
baby.latch_tip: Switch breasts every feed — start with the breast you ended on last time. This ensures both sides are stimulated equally.

mom.focus: Engorgement usually peaks and then eases by today. Keep feeding frequently. Celebrate — you've made it to day 5.

breakfast.name: Whole wheat pancakes (tawa roti) with ghee and jaggery
breakfast.why: Whole wheat provides sustained energy. Jaggery's iron is absorbed better than processed sugar.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Simple atta paratha with a thick coating of ghee. No need for anything fancy.

lunch.name: Chana dal with gourd (turai/ridge gourd) + rice
lunch.why: Chana dal is protein + fiber. Ridge gourd is cooling and reduces post-birth inflammation.
lunch.recipe_tip: Pressure cook chana dal. Add sautéed turai with cumin, turmeric. No red chilli.

dinner.name: Egg curry (soft-cooked) + rice (non-veg) / Rajma (kidney beans) + rice (veg)
dinner.why: Complete proteins. Eggs especially are the easiest bioavailable protein for postpartum recovery.
dinner.recipe_tip: For egg curry: poach eggs in a simple onion-tomato-turmeric gravy. Avoid heavy spices.

snacks: Halim seeds soaked in water + jaggery

foods_to_avoid: Fermented foods (idli, dosa) are fine for South Indian moms, but watch if baby seems gassy
mom.hydration_tip: Today is a good day to add fresh coconut water (room temperature) — hydrating, potassium-rich, and supports blood pressure.
mom.traditional_note: Day 5 is when many families do the first ritual bath for the mother in many Indian traditions — warm water infused with neem leaves or herbal mixtures. This is wonderful for skin and mood.

baby.what_to_expect: Breastmilk is now fully established. Baby's weight loss should be stopping and recovery beginning. Baby may have a longer sleep stretch of 2–3 hours at night.
signs_feeding_well: Bright yellow mustard stools — seedy texture is normal
signs_to_watch: Still losing weight after day 5 — speak to your paediatrician
Sunken fontanelle (soft spot on head that looks dipped) — sign of dehydration
baby.latch_tip: If your nipples are sore, apply a few drops of expressed breastmilk after each feed and let it air-dry. Breastmilk has antibacterial properties that help heal cracked nipples faster than any cream.

mom.focus: You may feel emotionally low today — this is the 'baby blues' peak. It is hormonal, normal, and almost always resolves by day 10. You are not failing.

breakfast.name: Warm oats porridge with ghee, jaggery, cardamom
breakfast.why: Oats are the most well-researched galactagogue (milk-booster). Cardamom uplifts mood.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Use rolled oats, not instant. Cook with milk, not just water. Add ghee + cardamom + jaggery.

lunch.name: Mixed dal (toor + moong) + spinach sabzi + rice
lunch.why: Mixed dals cover a broader amino acid profile. Spinach iron supports energy on emotional days.
lunch.recipe_tip: Mix cooked toor and moong dal. Add wilted spinach. Simple garlic-ghee tadka.

dinner.name: Warm kheer (rice pudding) + soft dal + roti
dinner.why: Kheer is comfort food — serotonin-boosting carbs + warm milk. Exactly what you need on a hard emotional day.
dinner.recipe_tip: Make with full-fat milk, basmati rice, cardamom, saffron if available. Ghee on roti.

snacks: Saffron milk (2–3 strands saffron in warm milk) — mood-lifting

foods_to_avoid: Avoid any foods that gave baby gas in last 2 days
Limit tea — caffeine worsens anxiety on emotional days
mom.hydration_tip: Saffron water — 3 strands saffron in warm water — is a traditional mood-lifter backed by modern research. Drink once daily.
mom.traditional_note: Baby blues peak around day 3–5 and ease by day 10 in most women. This is different from postpartum depression. If low mood persists beyond 2 weeks or feels severe, please speak to your doctor.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby's eyes may look a little puffy or crusty — this is from the antibiotic eye drops given at birth. Clean gently with warm water and a soft cloth. Baby is becoming more alert between feeds.
signs_feeding_well: Baby makes eye contact briefly during feeding
signs_to_watch: Yellow eyes (whites of the eyes look yellow) — check for jaundice
Baby extremely sleepy, hard to wake
baby.latch_tip: Try feeding in a quiet room with low light for evening feeds — it helps baby (and you) settle faster and reduces overtiredness.

mom.focus: One full week. You've done it. Your milk is established. Your body is beginning to heal. Celebrate today.

breakfast.name: Poha with peanuts + a glass of warm milk
breakfast.why: Poha is iron-fortified, light, and easy. Peanuts add protein. A complete feel-good breakfast.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Rinse poha, drain. Sauté with mustard, curry leaves, onion, turmeric. Top with lemon + coriander.

lunch.name: Dal makhani (mild) + rice + cucumber raita
lunch.why: Dal makhani is protein-rich and comforting. A week in, you can introduce mild spice and raita.
lunch.recipe_tip: Use very little cream. Skip heavy kasuri methi. Cook the dal very soft.

dinner.name: Fish curry + rice (non-veg) / Kadhi pakora + rice (veg)
dinner.why: Fish (especially rohu or pomfret) gives DHA — critical for baby's brain development through breastmilk. Kadhi (yoghurt curry) supports gut health.
dinner.recipe_tip: Fish curry: light turmeric + coriander base. Avoid too much tamarind today.

snacks: Fresh fruit — banana, chiku, papaya (fully ripe)

foods_to_avoid: Papaya that isn't fully ripe — raw/semi-ripe papaya can affect milk supply
Heavy fried snacks
mom.hydration_tip: Fenugreek tea: boil 1 tsp methi seeds in 2 cups water for 10 minutes, strain, add honey. Drink once daily — well-researched for increasing milk supply.
mom.traditional_note: Many families do a 'naming ceremony' or puja on day 7 in various Indian traditions. Let the family handle the arrangements — your only job is to rest and feed.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby has usually regained birth weight by day 7–10. Cord stump is drying and will fall off in the next few days. Baby is more awake and responsive. This is the beginning of social connection.
signs_feeding_well: Weight at or approaching birth weight
Baby tracks your face with their eyes
signs_to_watch: Cord stump looks red, smells bad, or has discharge — see paediatrician
Baby hasn't regained birth weight by day 10
baby.latch_tip: One week in, your nipples should not be hurting after the first 30 seconds of a feed. If they are, get a latch check from a lactation consultant — most pain is preventable with a small adjustment.

mom.focus: Mature milk is now fully established. Focus on galactagogue foods that support supply. You can very slowly add a bit more variety.

breakfast.name: Methi paratha (fenugreek flatbread) with curd
breakfast.why: Methi paratha is the best all-round breakfast for breastfeeding moms — iron, calcium, and prolactin support in one.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Mix 1 tsp methi powder into atta dough. Make thicker parathas. Serve with plain curd (not chilled).

lunch.name: Palak paneer + roti + dal
lunch.why: Paneer = protein + calcium. Spinach = iron. This combo is perfect for rebuild week.
lunch.recipe_tip: Light palak paneer — no heavy cream. Use 1 tsp ghee, cumin, garlic, blanched spinach, soft paneer cubes.

dinner.name: Drumstick (moringa) dal + rice
dinner.why: Moringa leaves have 3x more iron than spinach, 4x more calcium than milk. Week 2 is when to start loading on it.
dinner.recipe_tip: Pressure cook toor dal. Add drumstick pods OR drumstick leaf paste. Coconut-based tadka for South Indian version.

snacks: Oats energy balls with methi and jaggery
Sprouted moong salad (lightly stir-fried, not raw)

foods_to_avoid: Raw sprouts — still avoid raw until week 3
Fizzy drinks
mom.hydration_tip: Moringa tea: steep dried moringa leaves in hot water for 5 minutes. Mild in flavour, incredibly nutrient-dense. One cup daily.
mom.traditional_note: In Karnataka and Tamil Nadu, drumstick sambar every day during weeks 1–6 is the traditional prescription. It's backed by nutrition science.

baby.what_to_expect: Mature milk is white and may look thin/watery — this is completely normal. The first milk (foremilk) is thin and hydrating; the later milk (hindmilk) is creamier and more calorie-dense. Baby needs both.
signs_feeding_well: Baby is alert and active between feeds
signs_to_watch: Green, frothy stools — may indicate foremilk-hindmilk imbalance (too many short feeds)
Baby seems hungry very soon after feeding
baby.latch_tip: To ensure baby gets hindmilk: feed from one breast until baby spontaneously pulls off or falls asleep. Only then offer the second breast. Don't switch every few minutes.

mom.focus: Engorgement easing. Keep eating warm, nourishing foods. Rest when baby rests.

breakfast.name: Methi paratha + warm milk
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Toor dal + lauki sabzi + rice
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Chicken broth + rice (non-veg) / Paneer khichdi (veg)
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Cold drinks
Raw vegetables
mom.hydration_tip: Saunf water sipped warm through the day. Fennel reduces infant gas passed through milk.
mom.traditional_note: Massage with warm sesame oil is traditionally given daily in weeks 1–2. Accept it — it genuinely aids recovery.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is getting more efficient at feeding — shorter feeds are normal as baby and you sync up.
signs_to_watch: Sudden weight plateau
Jaundice spreading below chest
baby.latch_tip: Hold baby's head at breast level — if you have to bend your nipple down to baby, your position needs adjusting.

mom.focus: Baby blues should be lifting by now. If you're still feeling very low, talk to someone. Your emotional health is as important as your physical recovery.

breakfast.name: Vegetable daliya (broken wheat porridge) with ghee
breakfast.why: Daliya is high in fibre, reduces constipation (a common post-birth issue). Warming and filling.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Pressure cook broken wheat with mixed vegetables, cumin, turmeric. Generous ghee on top.

lunch.name: Rajma (kidney beans) + rice
lunch.why: Rajma is your best plant-based complete protein. High in folate and iron.
lunch.recipe_tip: Soak overnight, pressure cook well, make a simple onion-tomato base. Avoid too much garam masala.

dinner.name: Light chicken soup with rice (non-veg) / Lauki chana dal (veg)
dinner.why: Chicken soup supports collagen production for healing. Lauki chana dal is gentle and protein-rich.
dinner.recipe_tip: Chicken soup: slow-cook with ginger, garlic, black pepper, turmeric. Add rice towards the end.

snacks: Anjeer (fig) + milk — highest plant calcium source

foods_to_avoid: Avoid chocolate for now — caffeine can disrupt baby's sleep
mom.hydration_tip: Milk thistle tea or ashwagandha warm milk (with doctor's advice) can support mood and energy in week 2.
mom.traditional_note: Day 10 is a common milestone in many Indian traditions — some families do a small pooja or ritual to mark the end of the first week+ of confinement.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby should be at or above birth weight by today. Cord stump has likely fallen off. Baby is having more wakeful, alert periods. First real social smiles are still weeks away, but baby is listening to your voice intently.
signs_feeding_well: 3–4 yellow stools daily (or one large one — both normal)
signs_to_watch: Umbilical area looks red or swollen after cord falls off
Baby arching back during feeds — may indicate reflux
baby.latch_tip: Burp baby in the middle of a feed (when they naturally pause) and at the end. At this age, babies swallow a lot of air. A mid-feed burp reduces spit-up.

mom.focus: Week 2 energy. Focus on iron-rich foods every day — your reserves are still rebuilding.

breakfast.name: Ragi dosa + sambar
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Palak dal + rice + ghee
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Khichdi + papad
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Excessive tea/coffee
Processed packaged snacks
mom.hydration_tip: Moringa leaf tea once daily. The mineral density of moringa is unmatched.
mom.traditional_note: Oil massage (champi) to hair and scalp is given in most Indian traditions in week 2. Sesame oil is preferred for its warming properties.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby may have a brief 10-day growth spurt — increased feeding frequency for 1–2 days. Normal and temporary.
signs_to_watch: Green frothy stools (foremilk imbalance)
Baby arching back during feeds
baby.latch_tip: Foremilk-hindmilk balance: let baby fully drain one breast before offering the second.

mom.focus: You may start to feel more like yourself. Don't overdo it — rest is still medicine.

breakfast.name: Oats porridge with dry fruits and ghee
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Rajma + rice (pressure-cooked very soft)
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Suji upma + raita
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Heavy fried snacks
Very spicy preparations
mom.hydration_tip: Dates soaked in warm water overnight — drink the water in the morning. Natural electrolytes and iron.
mom.traditional_note: Day 12 is when many North Indian families begin Panjeeri distribution. Eat at least 1–2 tablespoons daily.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby's sleep cycles are about 45 minutes. They may wake after one cycle and need resettling. This is normal brain development, not hunger.
signs_to_watch: Baby seeming very uncomfortable and gassy — track maternal diet
Persistent yellow skin
baby.latch_tip: If you have fast let-down and baby chokes at the start of feeds, try laid-back feeding position to slow flow.

mom.focus: Tomorrow is the 2-week mark. A true milestone. Your milk supply is established and your body is healing well.

breakfast.name: Methi thepla + curd
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Chana dal + dudhi (bottle gourd) + rice
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Moong dal soup + roti
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Carbonated drinks
Heavily processed food
mom.hydration_tip: Jeera water: boil 1 tsp cumin in 2 cups water, cool to warm. Best digestive drink for postpartum.
mom.traditional_note: Day 13 — many families complete the first two-week prayers/rituals. Accept blessings graciously.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is nearly at 2 weeks. Cord stump has likely fallen off. Baby's skin is normalising after peeling phase.
signs_to_watch: Umbilical area redness or smell
Baby seems to not recognise your face — very unusual at this stage
baby.latch_tip: Two-week check point: is feeding comfortable? If you haven't resolved latch pain yet, today is a good day to see a lactation consultant.

mom.focus: Two weeks in — a major milestone. Milk supply is well established. You can now slowly reintroduce more foods. Trust your body.

breakfast.name: Idli with sambar and coconut chutney (South) / Besan chilla (North)
breakfast.why: Fermented idli improves gut microbiome — good for both you and baby via breastmilk. Besan (gram flour) is protein-dense.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Watch if baby seems gassy for 12 hours after you eat fermented foods. Most babies are fine.

lunch.name: Full thali — dal, sabzi, rice, roti, curd
lunch.why: At 2 weeks, a balanced full thali is appropriate. You need variety for complete nutrition.
lunch.recipe_tip: Include a green leafy vegetable in every lunch from now on. Variety is your nutrition strategy.

dinner.name: Khichdi + pickle (small amount) + papad
dinner.why: Light, comforting, and easily digestible. A good dinner when you're exhausted from a full day.
dinner.recipe_tip: Khichdi should always have a generous spoon of ghee — it aids absorption of fat-soluble vitamins.

snacks: Fresh fruit — mango if in season, papaya (fully ripe), banana

foods_to_avoid: Still avoid very spicy or excessively oily food
Limit alcohol completely while breastfeeding
mom.hydration_tip: Nimbu pani with black salt (room temperature) is now fine — it replenishes electrolytes and vitamin C.
mom.traditional_note: Day 14 marks the end of 'strict confinement' in many Indian families. A warm bath with herbal water, a massage, and a small celebration are traditional. You've earned it.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is growing rapidly — gaining 150–200g per week. Baby is more aware of surroundings. May startle at loud sounds. First real smile is still a few weeks away but baby's face is becoming more expressive.
signs_feeding_well: Baby locks eyes with you during feeding
signs_to_watch: Baby seems very uncomfortable — pulling legs up, crying after feeds (possible colic or reflux)
Very infrequent stools (less than once in 3 days)
baby.latch_tip: At 2 weeks, if breastfeeding is still painful or difficult, please don't push through alone. A lactation consultant visit now can resolve 90% of latch issues in one session.

mom.focus: Phase 3 begins. You can slowly add more variety. Your digestive system is recovering. Your energy is returning.

breakfast.name: Besan chilla with vegetables
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Mixed dal + rice + spinach sabzi
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Paneer curry + roti (veg) / Egg curry + rice (non-veg)
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Very spicy restaurant food — still rebuild gradually
mom.hydration_tip: Hibiscus-tulsi herbal tea — cooling, iron-rich, mood-supportive. One cup daily.
mom.traditional_note: Week 3 is when gentle movement begins in traditional recovery. Short, slow walks within the house are appropriate.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is becoming more socially aware. Responds to your voice with increased activity. First real smiles are approaching.
signs_to_watch: Sudden change in stool frequency or colour
Baby seems unwell — fever in a baby this young is always a doctor visit
baby.latch_tip: Experiment with different feeding positions for comfort. The best position is the one that is comfortable for both of you.

mom.focus: Emotional peaks and valleys are normal at this stage. Hormones are still recalibrating. Be patient with yourself.

breakfast.name: Poha with peanuts and pomegranate seeds
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Kadhi pakora + jeera rice
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Vegetable soup + toast
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Alcohol — not while breastfeeding
Excess caffeine on emotional days
mom.hydration_tip: Saffron water: 3–4 strands in warm water. Saffron's safranal compound has clinical evidence for mood support.
mom.traditional_note: Day 16 — in many families the oil bath continues. Warm sesame oil massage before bath is deeply restorative for postpartum back and joint pain.

baby.what_to_expect: Three-week growth spurt may begin around day 17–21. Watch for sudden increase in feeding frequency.
signs_to_watch: Your own mood — if persistent sadness, anxiety, or feeling disconnected from baby, speak to your doctor
baby.latch_tip: Try skin-to-skin during one feed daily even at this stage. Oxytocin released during skin contact boosts supply.

mom.focus: You should be feeling some energy returning. C-section moms: your incision is healing — watch for any redness or discharge. Normal delivery moms: perineal stitches should be much more comfortable.

breakfast.name: Banana oat smoothie (warm or room temperature)
breakfast.why: Bananas are rich in B6 which supports mood regulation. Oats boost milk supply. A quick breakfast for busy mornings.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Blend ripe banana + cooked oats + milk + 1 tsp ghee + cardamom. Warm slightly on stove or drink at room temp.

lunch.name: Chole (chickpeas) + bhatura/roti + raita
lunch.why: Chickpeas are the highest-protein legume. Full of iron and zinc.
lunch.recipe_tip: Bhatura is fine once a week now. Use well-cooked chole with moderate spice. Raita cools it down.

dinner.name: Grilled fish + steamed vegetables + rice (non-veg) / Tofu stir-fry + rice (veg)
dinner.why: Week 3 allows more variety. Fish + vegetables is a nutrient-dense, light dinner.
dinner.recipe_tip: Season fish with turmeric, coriander, cumin. Grill or pan-fry in minimal oil. Avoid deep frying.

snacks: Dark chocolate (1–2 squares) — you've earned it

foods_to_avoid: If baby develops rash or unusual fussiness, track what you ate 4–6 hours earlier
mom.hydration_tip: You can now drink room-temperature water. The strict 'warm water only' phase is over for most moms unless you specifically feel better with warm water.
mom.traditional_note: Week 3 is when most Indian families do the 'Naamkaran' (naming ceremony). The emotional joy of this event is wonderful medicine — participate fully.

baby.feeds_per_day: 8–10 feeds — watch for growth spurt feeds
baby.what_to_expect: First growth spurt often happens around 2–3 weeks. Baby will feed constantly for 1–2 days, then settle back. This is temporary, normal, and necessary for supply increase. It feels overwhelming but it passes.
signs_feeding_well: Baby is growing visibly — cheeks filling out
Baby has more awake, alert time
signs_to_watch: Baby's poop is suddenly red-tinged — can indicate sensitivity to dairy in your diet
Baby seems in pain during or after feeds
baby.latch_tip: During a growth spurt, the urge to supplement with formula is very strong. Resist if possible — your supply will catch up within 24–48 hours of frequent feeding. Supplementing can actually reduce supply.

mom.focus: You're past the midpoint. Strength is returning. The hardest nights are behind you.

breakfast.name: Daliya (broken wheat) khichdi with ghee
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Moong sprout sabzi (lightly cooked) + dal + rice
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Fish curry + rice (non-veg) / Tofu curry + rice (veg)
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Watching what consistently makes baby gassy — make your own elimination list
mom.hydration_tip: Coconut milk: add to your chai instead of regular milk occasionally. Supports energy and lactation.
mom.traditional_note: Week 3 is when family helpers typically transition. Be honest about what support you still need.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby enjoys being talked to and will coo back. These 'conversations' are your baby's first language development.
signs_to_watch: Baby has developed a flat spot on head — reposition regularly during sleep and tummy time
baby.latch_tip: If baby seems distracted during feeds, try humming or softly singing — familiar sounds help baby focus.

mom.focus: Focus on protein at every meal — muscle recovery and milk production both require it.

breakfast.name: Greek-style curd with honey and nuts
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Chole + roti + raita
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Grilled chicken + steamed rice + salad (non-veg) / Paneer tikka + rice (veg)
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Skipping meals — your body needs consistent fuel for milk production
mom.hydration_tip: Protein shake: blend 1 cup milk + 1 banana + 1 tbsp nut butter + cardamom. Natural, no powder needed.
mom.traditional_note: Naamkaran (naming ceremony) often happens around week 3. The ritual joy and family gathering supports postpartum recovery.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby's nervous system is maturing. They may seem more unsettled in the evenings — this is 'witching hour' and is very common.
signs_to_watch: Witching hour crying (6–10pm) lasting 3+ hours for multiple days may be colic
baby.latch_tip: Evening cluster feeding during witching hour is normal — baby is building your evening supply. It will ease.

mom.focus: Three weeks tomorrow. The 'fourth trimester' survival mode is easing. You're moving into a rhythm.

breakfast.name: Methi paratha + curd + pickle
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Sambhar + idli + chutney (South) / Dal + rice + pickle (North)
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Light khichdi + papad
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Skimping on ghee — fat-soluble vitamins A, D, E, K all depend on it
mom.hydration_tip: Warm milk + ashwagandha powder (with doctor's guidance) — supports energy and stress response.
mom.traditional_note: Day 20 — five days to the 3-week mark. Acknowledge your progress out loud today.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is beginning to distinguish between day and night, but may not reliably 'sleep at night' for several more weeks. This is developmental, not a problem.
signs_feeding_well: Baby is tracking moving objects with their eyes
signs_to_watch: If baby's eyes appear crossed consistently — mention at next paediatrician visit (some eye crossing is normal early on)
baby.latch_tip: Practice responsive feeding — feed when baby shows hunger cues (sucking hands, rooting) rather than crying. Earlier response = less stressed feed.

mom.focus: Three weeks. Your body has done something extraordinary. Be gentle with yourself — physically and emotionally.

breakfast.name: Dosa + sambar + coconut chutney
breakfast.why: Full South Indian breakfast is now perfect. Fermented dosa supports gut health. Sambar has all your vegetables.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Classic thin crispy dosa. Make the sambar with drumstick, carrot, tomato, and a generous tamarind-lentil base.

lunch.name: Ghee rice + chicken curry (non-veg) / Dal tadka + jeera rice (veg)
lunch.why: Ghee rice is deeply nourishing — fat-soluble vitamins from ghee, carbs from rice. A celebration-worthy lunch.
lunch.recipe_tip: Ghee rice: fry whole spices (cardamom, clove, bay leaf) in ghee, add rice and water 1:1.5.

dinner.name: Light soup + toast + fruit
dinner.why: Keep dinner light for better sleep. Heavy dinners disrupt the already-fragmented sleep of a breastfeeding mother.
dinner.recipe_tip: Tomato shorba or mixed vegetable soup with whole wheat toast. Fruit salad (room temperature).

snacks: Makhana (fox nut) in ghee + rock salt

foods_to_avoid: No specific restrictions now — use common sense and watch baby's reaction
mom.hydration_tip: At 3 weeks, you can resume coffee — but limit to 1 cup per day and drink it right after a feed (not before) to minimize caffeine in breastmilk.
mom.traditional_note: Three weeks is often when the 'helper' leaves. This is the hardest transition point for many Indian mothers — going from supported to alone. Plan this in advance. Ask your partner to take leave if possible.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is growing rapidly and becoming more interactive. Eye contact is more sustained. Baby may begin to recognize your smell, voice, and face distinctly. Second growth spurt may happen around 3 weeks.
signs_feeding_well: Baby smiles responsively at your face (not just gas-smiles)
signs_to_watch: Persistent crying for 3+ hours per day — may indicate colic
Baby refusing one breast consistently
baby.latch_tip: If baby consistently refuses one breast, that side may have a slower flow or different nipple angle. Try different feeding positions on the 'refused' side — football hold often works when cradle hold doesn't.

mom.focus: Phase 4. Full diet is appropriate. Focus on quality and variety.

breakfast.name: Vegetable upma + coconut chutney
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Your choice — no restrictions in phase 4
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Light soup + roti + sabzi
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Very high-mercury fish
Alcohol while breastfeeding
mom.hydration_tip: Aim for 2.5–3L total fluids daily. Track by keeping a water bottle nearby at all feeds.
mom.traditional_note: Week 4 is often when the final family helper leaves. Have a plan for support.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is 3+ weeks — you know their patterns now. Trust your knowledge.
signs_to_watch: Your own energy levels — are you getting any sleep?
Watch for signs of mastitis if you feel flu-like symptoms
baby.latch_tip: Mastitis early signs: one breast is red, hot, painful, and you feel feverish. Feed more frequently on that side and see a doctor immediately.

mom.focus: Week 4. You've crossed into familiar territory — the acute recovery phase is behind you.

breakfast.name: Egg bhurji + toast (non-veg) / Paneer bhurji + paratha (veg)
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Biryani or pulao — you've earned it
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Curd rice + vegetable curry
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Nothing — use good judgement and enjoy food
mom.hydration_tip: Nimbu pani with black salt is back on the menu and delicious.
mom.traditional_note: If family has a tradition of first outing with baby at 3–4 weeks, this is the week for it.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby will have first vaccines at 6 weeks. Baby may be fussier after vaccines — extra feeds and cuddles are the best medicine.
signs_to_watch: Prepare a list of questions for the 6-week paediatrician appointment
baby.latch_tip: A well-fed baby at 3+ weeks feeds for 10–15 minutes per side efficiently. Duration of feed is no longer a reliable indicator of intake.

mom.focus: Notice your emotional state today. Week 4 PPD risk is real. How are you feeling beyond 'fine'?

breakfast.name: Banana pancakes + ghee
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Fish curry + rice (non-veg) / Palak paneer + roti (veg)
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Khichdi + warm milk
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Isolating yourself — reach out to a friend or family member today
mom.hydration_tip: Rose milk (warm) — rose is associated with heart-opening and emotional wellbeing in Ayurveda.
mom.traditional_note: Check in with yourself: beyond the physical, how is your emotional health? Postpartum depression is common and treatable. Asking for help is strength.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby's personality is emerging clearly. You can tell the difference between all their cries. This knowledge is hard-won.
signs_to_watch: If you feel persistently anxious, sad, or unlike yourself — please speak to your doctor today. PPD is at its peak risk around weeks 4–6.
baby.latch_tip: At 3.5 weeks, consider attending a local breastfeeding support group (or online group) — community with other nursing mothers is incredibly supportive.

mom.focus: You're over the hardest part. Full diet is now appropriate for most moms. Focus on quality — not restriction.

breakfast.name: Egg bhurji + toast (non-veg) / Paneer bhurji + paratha (veg)
breakfast.why: Eggs or paneer are complete proteins that give you sustained energy for the morning.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Keep the bhurji moderately spiced. Avoid chillies if baby still seems sensitive.

lunch.name: Any regional favourite — biryani, pulao, full thali
lunch.why: Week 4 is when you can eat 'normally' again. Your digestive system is recovered. The 40-day protocol is about to complete.
lunch.recipe_tip: Biryani: choose lighter versions (yakhni pulao, vegetable biryani). Keep portions moderate.

dinner.name: Light sabzi + dal + roti
dinner.why: Keep dinners lighter than lunches — helps with sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Simple home dal. A sabzi of your choice. Two rotis with ghee. Simple is perfect.

foods_to_avoid: Very high-mercury fish (shark, swordfish, tuna) — limit to once a week
Excessive alcohol — limit or avoid while breastfeeding
mom.hydration_tip: Daily target: 2.5–3 litres of fluids total (water, milk, soups, nimbu pani). Breastfeeding requires extra hydration.
mom.traditional_note: Week 4 is when many families do the first outing for mother and baby — a temple visit, a walk in the neighbourhood. Fresh air and sunlight are genuinely healing.

baby.feeds_per_day: 7–9 feeds — getting a bit more efficient
baby.what_to_expect: Baby is becoming a more efficient feeder — feeds that used to take 45 minutes now take 10–15 minutes. Baby is awake for longer stretches. May have started a loose day/night rhythm.
signs_feeding_well: Baby is alert and content for 1–2 hour stretches
signs_to_watch: Feeding efficiency drops suddenly — may indicate illness like ear infection
White patches inside baby's mouth (thrush)
baby.latch_tip: As feeds get shorter, don't panic that baby isn't getting enough. An efficient 10-minute feed at 4 weeks delivers more milk than a sleepy 40-minute feed at day 3.

mom.focus: Almost at one month. A profound milestone. Your body has done extraordinary things.

breakfast.name: Whole wheat toast with avocado and eggs (non-veg) / Nut butter on toast (veg)
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Dal + sabzi + rice + roti + curd
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Soup + bread + salad
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

snacks: Your choice — you know your body

foods_to_avoid: Skipping meals under any circumstances — milk production suffers immediately
mom.hydration_tip: Coconut water daily from this point — electrolytes support energy during the demanding fourth week.
mom.traditional_note: Many families mark one month with a small pooja or family gathering. It's a beautiful moment to receive blessings.

baby.what_to_expect: One-month-old baby is a completely different being from the newborn of day 1. Interactive, expressive, and deeply bonded with you.
signs_to_watch: Prepare vaccine records for the 6-week visit
Note any questions about baby's development
baby.latch_tip: By one month, most breastfeeding challenges are manageable or resolved. If you're still struggling — please get help. It is available and it works.

mom.focus: One month postpartum today. Congratulations. You are a different person than you were 27 days ago.

breakfast.name: Special celebratory breakfast
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Festive lunch with family
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Your favourite comfort dinner
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Nothing — eat with gratitude and joy
mom.hydration_tip: Celebrate with your favourite drink — nimbu pani, coconut water, or a special herbal drink.
mom.traditional_note: One month is celebrated in many Indian cultures. Accept all blessings and food with grace.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is one month old! First proper social smiles are imminent if they haven't appeared yet.
signs_feeding_well: Both of you have grown enormously
signs_to_watch: Upcoming 6-week vaccination — prepare yourself emotionally (it's harder on the parent than the baby)
baby.latch_tip: One month of breastfeeding — whether it has been easy or hard, you have given your baby something irreplaceable. Be proud.

mom.focus: Four weeks. Your 6-week check-up is coming. Start thinking about what questions you want to ask your doctor.

breakfast.name: Upma / Poha / Paratha — your choice
breakfast.why: You know what works for you now. Trust yourself.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Stick with what gives you energy and doesn't make baby gassy.

lunch.name: Comfort food of your choice — eat what makes you happy
lunch.why: Nutrition and joy are not separate. A meal that makes you happy is nutritionally better than a perfect meal eaten with resentment.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook what you love. Ask family to cook your favourites.

dinner.name: Easy, light, stress-free dinner
dinner.why: The mental load of cooking plus feeding a baby is real. Simplify dinners.
dinner.recipe_tip: Curd rice + pickle is a complete, quick, gut-healthy dinner for exhausted evenings.

snacks: Nuts and seeds remain excellent daily habits

foods_to_avoid: Trust your own experience by now — you know what affects your baby
mom.hydration_tip: Coconut milk chai (replace regular milk with coconut milk) is a nice change — lower in lactose, higher in medium-chain fatty acids that support energy.
mom.traditional_note: Four weeks is a natural moment to take stock. How is your mental health? Are you getting any sleep? Do you have support? These questions matter as much as nutrition.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby may have their first genuine social smile around now — the full face-lights-up smile that is unmistakably directed at you. This is one of the most rewarding moments of early parenthood.
signs_feeding_well: Baby smiles at you during feeding pauses
signs_to_watch: Any sudden change in feeding pattern, sleep, or behaviour — trust your gut, see the doctor
Mama, watch your own mood too — PPD can appear at 4 weeks
baby.latch_tip: Four-week-old babies often get distracted during feeds — looking around, pulling off to see a sound. This is normal development. Feed in a quieter space for more efficient sessions.

mom.focus: Week 5 begins. The 40-day milestone is close. You're entering the final stretch.

breakfast.name: Oat banana smoothie bowl
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Grilled fish + rice + dal (non-veg) / Tofu stir fry + noodles (veg)
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Khichdi + sabzi
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Nothing specific — maintain the healthy habits you've built
mom.hydration_tip: Tulsi ginger tea — anti-inflammatory, immunity-boosting, and wonderfully warming.
mom.traditional_note: Week 5 confinement is easing in most families. Short outdoor walks in morning sun are recommended.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is going through rapid brain development. They are building memory — they will remember the melody you sing, the words you repeat.
signs_to_watch: Your own sleep — are you getting any stretches? Sleep deprivation at 5 weeks is a real health concern
baby.latch_tip: Morning feeds often have the richest milk — highest fat content because supply recalibrates overnight.

mom.focus: Thirty days. A profound number. You have spent 30 days doing one of the hardest things a human can do.

breakfast.name: Methi thepla + pickle + curd
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Your favourite regional meal
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Light soup + roti
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Nothing — trust yourself completely now
mom.hydration_tip: Water with mint and cucumber (room temperature) — refreshing and hydrating.
mom.traditional_note: Some families observe a 30-day ritual. Let tradition support you — don't let it exhaust you.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby at 30 days has tripled their synaptic connections from birth. The calm time you spend with them is literally building their brain.
signs_feeding_well: Baby responds to your face and voice with full engagement
signs_to_watch: Your breast health — any lumps, redness, or pain should be assessed. Blocked ducts are common at this stage.
baby.latch_tip: Blocked duct relief: massage firmly toward nipple during feeding on affected side. Warm compress before feed, cold compress after.

mom.focus: Nine days to the milestone. You are nearly there.

breakfast.name: Protein-rich breakfast of your choice
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Full thali lunch
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Light dinner
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Skipping sleep when the opportunity presents
mom.hydration_tip: Electrolyte water: water + pinch of salt + squeeze of lemon + pinch of jaggery. Natural sports drink.
mom.traditional_note: Preparations for the Day 40 celebration can begin. What ritual will your family observe?

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is becoming highly interactive. Babbling, cooing, and 'talking back' in their own language.
signs_to_watch: Prepare your 6-week checkup questions for yourself AND the 6-week paed appointment
baby.latch_tip: Consider pumping once a day to build a freezer stash if you plan to return to work.

mom.focus: Week 5. Recovery is substantially complete for most moms. C-section moms: your internal healing continues for 6–8 weeks total.

breakfast.name: South: pesarattu + ginger chutney | North: moong chilla + mint chutney
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Fish biryani (non-veg) / Vegetable biryani (veg)
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Soup + salad
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: C-section moms: avoid heavy lifting still. Check with your doctor at 6-week visit.
mom.hydration_tip: Cinnamon water: boil a cinnamon stick in 2 cups water. Anti-inflammatory and blood-sugar stabilizing.
mom.traditional_note: Week 5 — last week before the traditional Japa period ends. Begin preparing mentally for the transition back to regular life.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby may show first evidence of sleep consolidation — a slightly longer stretch at night (3–4 hours). Treasure it.
signs_feeding_well: Baby shows clear day/night preference beginning
signs_to_watch: If you're returning to work in weeks, start establishing a pumping schedule now
baby.latch_tip: If returning to work: pace bottle feeding. Have the caregiver use slow-flow nipple and paced bottle feeding to avoid nipple confusion.

mom.focus: Week 5, day 5. The body that grew and birthed a human is now almost fully recovered. Honour it.

breakfast.name: Granola with warm milk and fruit (Indian adaptation: muesli with warm milk)
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Dal + sabzi + rice + curd — simple and complete
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Paneer bhurji + roti (veg) / Egg bhurji + roti (non-veg)
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Nothing — eat with mindfulness and joy
mom.hydration_tip: Almond milk chai (blend soaked almonds into warm water + chai spices) — a luxurious variant for the penultimate week.
mom.traditional_note: Day 33 — 7 days to Japa completion. Begin any family preparations for the celebration.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is visibly bigger than at birth. Filling out, stronger, more responsive. You are the expert on your baby.
signs_to_watch: At 5+ weeks, if breastfeeding is still very difficult — please get support. It doesn't have to be this hard.
baby.latch_tip: Skin-to-skin contact at any age has measurable benefits — oxytocin, bonding, regulation. Never too old for it.

mom.focus: Almost there. Six more days. Your body and your baby are both doing beautifully.

breakfast.name: Special breakfast — treat yourself
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Your favourite lunch
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Light satisfying dinner
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Stress — as much as humanly possible with a 5-week-old
mom.hydration_tip: Lavender-hibiscus herbal infusion: steep dried hibiscus and lavender in hot water. Calming and iron-rich.
mom.traditional_note: Families often do a complete home cleaning and preparation ritual before the Day 40 ceremony.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby at 34 days knows you. Completely and deeply. They will show signs of distress if held by strangers — this is healthy attachment forming.
signs_to_watch: Note everything you want to ask at the 6-week appointments — yours and baby's
baby.latch_tip: The 40-day feeding journey so far has been one of the most physically and emotionally demanding things you've done. It is also one of the most profound.

mom.focus: Almost at the milestone. Five weeks of recovery, growth, and becoming a mother. You are different than you were 35 days ago — more capable than you know.

breakfast.name: Vermicelli upma + boiled egg (non-veg) / Semolina upma + coconut chutney (veg)
breakfast.why: Light, quick, filling — optimised for busy mornings with a 5-week-old.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Roast semolina/vermicelli in ghee first for better flavour. Add vegetables and roasted peanuts.

lunch.name: Dal + sabzi + rice + roti + salad
lunch.why: The full balanced Indian thali — your default should be this from now on.
lunch.recipe_tip: Include one raw element now (cucumber, tomato, carrot) — digestive system is fully recovered.

dinner.name: Khichdi or soup + roti — light ending to the day
dinner.why: Light dinners help you fall back asleep faster after night feeds.
dinner.recipe_tip: 5-spice khichdi: cumin, coriander, turmeric, ginger, garlic. Simple and deeply nourishing.

foods_to_avoid: No restrictions except personal sensitivities and high-mercury fish
mom.hydration_tip: Herbal teas (tulsi, ginger, cinnamon) are a wonderful daily habit to maintain — anti-inflammatory and supports overall immunity.
mom.traditional_note: Day 35 — five weeks. In many traditions, the mother starts resuming light household activities. Listen to your body. Don't rush. Your recovery timeline is your own.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby is becoming a full personality. Distinct crying patterns for hunger, tiredness, and discomfort. Baby enjoys being talked to and will vocalize back. Tummy time is important from now — a few minutes daily.
signs_feeding_well: Steady weight gain week on week
signs_to_watch: Baby not gaining weight despite feeding frequently — supply check needed
Baby very stiff or very floppy during handling
baby.latch_tip: At 5 weeks, you and baby are a well-practiced team. Trust the process. If something feels off, it probably is — seek help without hesitation.

mom.focus: Phase 5 begins. Four days to the milestone. Begin the gentle celebration.

breakfast.name: Celebration-worthy breakfast — puri halwa (North) / Dosa + all chutneys (South)
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Festive meal with family
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Light khichdi
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Nothing — full food freedom is your reward
mom.hydration_tip: Rose + cardamom milk: warm milk with rose water and cardamom. Celebratory and deeply nourishing.
mom.traditional_note: Day 36 begins the final phase in Indian tradition. In some families, gifts are given to the new mother. The community acknowledges her journey.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby at 36 days is a complete little person. Their personality is visible. Their preferences are clear. Their bond with you is unbreakable.
signs_to_watch: 4 days to the milestone — write down 3 things you are proud of from this journey
baby.latch_tip: Day 36 latch tip: you don't need tips anymore. You have earned your expertise.

mom.focus: Three days to Day 40. What do you want to carry forward from this journey? What do you want to leave behind?

breakfast.name: Warm oats with ghee + cardamom + jaggery
breakfast.why: Nourishing choice for this phase of recovery.

lunch.name: Your choice — whatever makes you happy
lunch.why: Balanced and appropriate for your recovery stage.
lunch.recipe_tip: Cook with ghee and warming spices.

dinner.name: Light, early dinner
dinner.why: Light and easily digestible for better sleep quality.
dinner.recipe_tip: Keep it simple — exhaustion is real.

foods_to_avoid: Nothing
mom.hydration_tip: Turmeric golden milk + black pepper + ghee. Anti-inflammatory, healing, and ceremonially appropriate for the final days.
mom.traditional_note: Day 37 is when many families begin gathering for the Day 40 celebration. Grandmothers, aunts, and community come together.

baby.what_to_expect: Baby at 37 days is ready to meet the world in a limited way. Careful, short outings in clean, uncrowded environments are appropriate.
signs_feeding_well: The bond between you is visible to everyone
signs_to_watch: Two more days. You have this.
baby.latch_tip: Three days to go. You are a breastfeeding mother. Whatever comes next in your feeding journey, you have given your baby something that cannot be replicated.

mom.focus: The home stretch. Day 40 is 2 days away. The traditional Japa period is almost complete. Reflect on how far you've come.

breakfast.name: Special halwa (wheat + ghee + nuts) or your favourite celebratory breakfast
breakfast.why: Day 38 calls for something special — you're nearly at the milestone.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Atta halwa: roast whole wheat flour in generous ghee, add sugar syrup, nuts. Warming and celebratory.

lunch.name: Festive meal of your choice — something you've been wanting
lunch.why: Eat with joy. You've earned every bite.
lunch.recipe_tip: Let family cook your favourite foods. Accept help graciously.

dinner.name: Light, comforting dinner before the big day
dinner.why: Rest well tonight — day 40 is your milestone.
dinner.recipe_tip: Dahi rice or khichdi — light, gut-friendly, easy.

snacks: Warm milk with haldi and ashwagandha (with doctor's guidance)

foods_to_avoid: Nothing is off-limits at this stage — eat with awareness and joy
mom.hydration_tip: Saffron milk — a full glass with saffron, cardamom, and warm milk — is the traditional Day 38–40 drink. Beautiful and restorative.
mom.traditional_note: Different regions celebrate day 40 differently. Bengalis have Shashthi (day 6) and other rituals. Tamil families have Seemantham. North Indian families often do a large pooja at 40 days. Ask your family what your tradition involves.

baby.what_to_expect: Your baby at 38 days is alert, expressive, and beginning to understand cause and effect. They know that crying brings you. They know your smell. They are forming the first attachment relationship of their life — with you.
signs_to_watch: Watch for postpartum depression in yourself — 6 weeks is a peak risk time
Baby's first vaccinations are at 6 weeks — prepare mentally and practically
baby.latch_tip: At 5+ weeks, breastfeeding becomes much more relaxed — you can feed lying down, while walking around, while talking to someone. Your body and baby have found their rhythm.

mom.focus: Tomorrow is Day 40. Whatever you feel — proud, exhausted, emotional, relieved — all of it is valid.

breakfast.name: Your choice — whatever feels most like you
breakfast.why: You know your body now. Feed it what it needs.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Make something you've been craving since before delivery.

lunch.name: A meal shared with family
lunch.why: Food tastes better in community. Let people around you today.
lunch.recipe_tip: Let someone else cook. You rest.

dinner.name: Early, light dinner for good rest before Day 40
dinner.why: Tomorrow is a milestone. Sleep as much as the baby allows.
dinner.recipe_tip: Curd rice + vegetable curry. Simple and complete.

foods_to_avoid: Nothing — eat with complete freedom and joy
mom.hydration_tip: Rose milk (warm milk with rose syrup and cardamom) is a beautiful pre-sleep drink for Day 39 evening.
mom.traditional_note: Day 39 evening: reflect on the last 40 days. Write down 3 things your body did that amazed you. You grew a human. You healed. You fed. You will carry this strength forever.

baby.what_to_expect: Your baby tomorrow will be 40 days old. In 40 days they have tripled their neural connections, doubled their wakefulness, and entirely reorganised your world. They know your voice, your smell, your heartbeat. This is the beginning of a lifelong relationship.
signs_to_watch: If anything concerns you, 6-week paediatrician visit is just days away — write down all your questions
baby.latch_tip: 40 days in, breastfeeding may feel completely natural — or it may still feel hard. Both are valid. The decision to continue, reduce, or stop is yours alone. There is no right answer except the one that works for your family.

mom.focus: Day 40. The Japa period is complete. You have done it. Your body has healed, your milk is flowing, your baby is thriving. Today, we celebrate you.

breakfast.name: Celebration breakfast — make it special
breakfast.why: Day 40 is your graduation day. Eat like it.
breakfast.recipe_tip: Puri + halwa + chana is the classic North Indian celebration breakfast. Dosa + all chutneys for South. Whatever your family makes to celebrate.

lunch.name: Full festive thali with all your favourites
lunch.why: A full, joyful meal shared with people you love is the best nutrition there is.
lunch.recipe_tip: Let the family cook everything. Your only job today is to eat, feed your baby, and receive love.

dinner.name: Whatever your heart desires
dinner.why: You have earned complete food freedom. Use it wisely and joyfully.
dinner.recipe_tip: If you feel like biryani, have biryani. If you want khichdi, have khichdi. There are no rules after today.

snacks: Tender coconut water — to begin the next phase fresh

foods_to_avoid: Nothing — today you eat with joy and zero restriction
mom.hydration_tip: Traditional Day 40 herbal drink: boil tulsi leaves, ginger, cinnamon, cardamom in water. Strain. Add honey and drink warm. This is your new chapter — clean, energised, and forward-facing.
mom.traditional_note: Day 40 — Japa complete. In Indian tradition, the 40-day period (Japa or confinement) officially ends today. You receive your first full bath with herbal water. You are blessed by elders. You step out of the home for the first time. You are a mother, fully initiated. Whatever comes next — trust yourself. You already know how.

baby.what_to_expect: Your 40-day-old baby is a miracle of development. They have social smiles, distinct vocalisations, strong neck muscles from tummy time, and a full personality. Their first vaccination visit is around 6 weeks. Solid foods begin at 6 months — you still have time.
signs_feeding_well: Your instincts are calibrated — trust them
signs_to_watch: Your own wellbeing — postpartum depression can peak at 6 weeks. If you feel persistently low, anxious, or unlike yourself — please reach out to your doctor. You deserve support too.
Baby's 6-week check-up — prepare your questions
baby.latch_tip: Day 40 latch tip: You don't need a tip anymore. You are the expert on your baby. If something doesn't feel right, you'll know. Trust that knowledge — it was hard-earned over 40 days.
